title: CleanSpark Reports Fiscal Year 2023 Financial Results
text: CleanSpark reported strong operational growth for fiscal year 2023 ending September 30, 2023. The company achieved revenue of $168.4 million, representing a 28% increase from the prior year's $131.5 million. However, the firm posted a net loss of $136.6 million compared to $57.3 million the previous year. Bitcoin production nearly doubled year-over-year, reaching 6,903 BTC mined during the fiscal period. The company surpassed a hashrate milestone of 10 exahashes per second, which management describes as among the most efficient in the industry. CEO Zach Bradford stated: We have not only met but exceeded many of our strategic aims and noted the company maintains the strongest balance sheet in our history. As of September 30, 2023, CleanSpark held: $29 million in cash, $56 million in bitcoin holdings, total assets of $762 million, stockholders equity of $677 million. By November 30, 2023, combined cash and bitcoin holdings increased to approximately $168 million, with debt reduced to $14.8 million. Adjusted EBITDA declined to $25.0 million from $56.1 million, primarily due to $32 million in accelerated depreciation expenses and $7 million in bitcoin impairment charges related to the company's expanded digital asset holdings.
